architecture.summary: Activation Addition (ActAdd) is an inference-time steering method that modifies a frozen language model's residual stream activations. The system computes a steering vector by taking the difference between forward-pass activations on a pair of contrast prompts (e.g., 'Love' minus 'Hate'). At inference, this vector is scaled by an injection coefficient and added to the residual stream at a specified layer, allowing the model to continue its forward pass with modified activations. No model weights are updated; only activations in the computational graph are perturbed.

biblio.title: Steering Language Models With Activation Engineering

claims.statement: ActAdd achieves state-of-the-art on negative-to-positive sentiment shift (0.669 success rate) on IMDb dataset using LLaMA-3-8B
ActAdd-OPT reduces toxicity to 0.112 on RealToxicityPrompts (n=1000), 8% lower than second-best method PREADD-D-OPT at 0.122
On wedding-topic steering with GPT-2-XL at optimal injection layer (layer 6), ActAdd achieves 90% success rate in generating wedding-related words compared to 2% baseline

baseline.name: gradient-based methods (Subramani et al. 2022, Hernandez et al. 2023)

claims.statement: ActAdd requires minimal data: steering vectors are computed from a single pair of contrast prompts (e.g., 'Love' vs 'Hate')
ActAdd preserves general model knowledge: on ConceptNet (LAMA benchmark), steering with the wedding vector has negligible impact on off-target answer probabilities across a range of top-k values

baseline.name: Subramani et al. 2022, Hernandez et al. 2023

claims.statement: ActAdd does not require machine optimization; steering vectors are computed by direct difference of activations without gradient descent or other iterative optimization

ActAdd requires access to intermediate activations at specified layers; the method is not immediately applicable given only API access to a model, as most APIs do not expose or cache intermediate activations

ActAdd introduces free hyperparameters (injection coefficient α and intervention layer ℓ) that must be tuned, making it less user-friendly than simple prompt engineering; the paper has not examined ActAdd's potential impact on reasoning capabilities
